content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 25, 1986


Cliff Stadig
6426 Zelzah Street
Reseda, CA  91335

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-86-205

Dear Mr. Stadig:

We are in receipt of your June 16, 1986, mailgram
requesting advice. The questions you have presented do not
fall under the purview of the Political Reform Act which this
agency administers.

The questions presented appear to fall under the Elections
Code. Your mailgram is thus being forwarded to the Office of
the Secretary of State for response.

Sincerely,

Jeanette E. Turvill
Legal Assistant
Legal Division

JET:plh
cc:  Secretary of State


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

CLIFF STADIG
6426 ZELZAH ST
RESEDA CA 91335 16PM

Western
Union Mailgram

4-0474288167 06/16/86 ICS IPMRNCZ CSP SACB JUN 17 9 07 AM '86
8187087204 MGMS TDRN RESEDA CA 126 06-16 0810P EST

►

THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICE COMMITTEE
428 J ST SUITE 800 PO BOX 807
SACRAMENTO CA 95804

TO THE COMMITTEE: PLEASE RETURN ANSWERS TO ME AS SOON AS POSSIBLE.
1. CAN POTENTIAL NEW CITIZENS STANDING OUTSIDE THE LOS ANGELES
CONVENTION CENTER IN LINE WAITING TO ENTER THE CONVENTION CENTER FOR
THEIR SWEARING IN CEREMONY, BE REGISTERED TO VOTE BEFORE ACTUALLY
BEING SWORN IN, EVEN THOUGH THEY MIGHT BE SWORN IN BEFORE THE END OF
THE HOUR?
2. CAN BOUNTY REGISTRATION PEOPLE PASS VOTER FORMS WITH THE RECIPT
PORTION (BOTTOM SECTION) ALREADY FILLED IN? THESE QUESTIONS NEED AN
EXACT ANSWERS TO AVOID EMBARRASSING SITUATIONS AND CONFUTATIONS
WHICH ARE APT TO DEVLOPMENT AT THESE NEW CITIZENS SWARING IN
CEREMONIES. SINCERELY YOURS
CLIFF STADIG
6426 ZELZAH ST
RESEDA CA 91335

20!10 EST

MGMCOMP

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the California Fair Political Practices Commission dated June 25, 1986, addressed to Cliff Stadig at 6426 Zelzah Street, Reseda, CA 91335. The letter responds to Mr. Stadig's request for advice, informing him that the questions presented do not fall under the purview of the Political Reform Act administered by this agency. Instead, they appear to fall under the Elections Code, and the mailgram is being forwarded to the Office of the Secretary of State for response. The letter is signed by Jeanette E. Turvill, Legal Assistant in the Legal Division, and includes the initials "JET:plh" and "cc: Secretary of State" at the bottom. The Western Union Mailgram is addressed to: THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICE COMMITTEE 428 J ST SUITE 800 PO BOX 807 SACRAMENTO CA 95804 The message from Cliff Stadig, located at 6426 Zelzah St, Reseda CA 91335, reads: TO THE COMMITTEE: PLEASE RETURN ANSWERS TO ME AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. 1. CAN POTENTIAL NEW CITIZENS STANDING OUTSIDE THE LOS ANGELES CONVENTION CENTER IN LINE WAITING TO ENTER THE CONVENTION CENTER FOR THEIR SWEARING IN CEREMONY, BE REGISTERED TO VOTE BEFORE ACTUALLY BEING SWORN IN. EVEN THOUGH THEY MIGHT BE SWORN IN BEFORE THE END OF THE HOUR? 2. CAN BOUNTY REGISTRATION PEOPLE PASS VOTER FORMS WITH THE RECEIPT PORTION (BOTTOM SECTION) ALREADY FILLED IN? THESE QUESTIONS NEED AN EXACT ANSWERS TO AVOID EMBARRASSING SITUATIONS AND CONFRONTATIONS WHICH ARE APT TO DEVELOPMENT AT THESE NEW CITIZENS SWARING IN CEREMONIES. SINCERELY YOURS CLIFF STADIG 6426 ZELZAH ST RESEDA CA 91335 20110 EST MGMCOMP
